Source_Species.reason: The paper studies apple and identifies MdCMS in apple fruit.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The title and text explicitly state Malus × domestica.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in apple and transient expression assays were done in Nicotiana benthamiana/tobacco systems; for MdCMS generally apple is clearly an applied species.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Apple is Malus × domestica; the main results are in this species.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Developmental, sequence, and many expression analyses were done in 'Jonagold'.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The gene was redesignated MdCMS after functional analysis demonstrated citramalate synthase activity.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the provided paper text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states MdCMS is similar to IPMS/MAM proteins, contains GxGERxG and HxH[DN]D motifs, and lacks the regulatory R-region present in IPMS.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper explicitly concludes MdCMS functions in a citramalate pathway contributing to isoleucine and ester biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results/Discussion describe MdCMS as the entry point into the citramalate pathway and link it to 1-C α-ketoacid elongation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Discussion explicitly states MdCMS_1 provides the entry point for carbon into the citramalate pathway.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Enzyme assays and pathway description show condensation of pyruvate with acetyl-CoA.

Primary_Product.reason: Assay products for MdCMS with pyruvate were verified as citramalate.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports condensation of pyruvate and acetyl-CoA to citramalate and reports MdCMS activity with α-ketobutyrate as well.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MdCMS is presented as supplying precursor carbon for isoleucine and ester biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper links the pathway to isoleucine, 2MB esters, and PROP esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These terminal products are described as an amino acid and volatile esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states isoleucine is a branched-chain amino acid and volatile esters contribute strongly to apple aroma/sensory quality.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Citramalate and related metabolites were measured in peel/skin tissues and volatiles in fruit headspace.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Multiple results support that MdCMS promotes citramalate accumulation and contributes to isoleucine, PROP, and 2MB ester formation during ripening.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Transient expression of MdCMS fused to YFP in tobacco indicated chloroplast targeting.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: MdCMS expression was primarily in ripening fruit, increasing from ripening onset and remaining high into senescence.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Semiquantitative RT-PCR showed MdCMS primarily expressed in ripening fruit and low/absent in leaf, root, and stem.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in Results/Methods: microarray, RT-PCR, enzyme assays/kinetics, feedback assays, 13C-feeding, transient expression, proteomics, and allelic association.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports MdCMS as a key entry-point enzyme, but does not explicitly demonstrate it is rate-limiting.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Ripening/senescence at room temperature and 13C-acetate feeding are explicitly described; ethylene dependence is suggested but not directly tested.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper supports reduced aroma-related ester production in lines with nonfunctional alleles; it does not establish other growth tradeoffs, matching the cautious phrasing.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper discusses allele effects and logical selection arguments, but does not directly test breeding or engineering applications.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Transient localization constructs used pEarleyGate101 with 35S; transient expression assays used Agrobacterium-mediated constructs in this framework.

Omics_Data.reason: Custom microarray, LC-MS/MS proteomics, and metabolite/isotopolog profiling are explicitly reported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All stated points appear in the paper: initial IPMS annotation, two Jonagold alleles, lack of R-region, and no BCAA inhibition.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This is an accurate synthesis of the paper's main MdCMS findings and is directly supported by Results and Conclusion.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given anywhere in the provided paper text.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study species is Malus × domestica.

Applied_Species.reason: MdCMS_1 was studied in apple and transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Malus × domestica and Nicotiana benthamiana are explicitly named.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Jonagold is specified for apple; no variety is given for N. benthamiana.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MdCMS_1 is the active citramalate synthase allele.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is provided in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MdCMS belongs to the IPMS/MAM/CMS-related family, lacks the R-region, and MdCMS_1 is the glutamine387 isozyme.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: MdCMS_1 is the apple citramalate pathway entry enzyme.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The paper links MdCMS_1 to the entry step and to 1-C α-ketoacid chain elongation reactions.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Discussion states MdCMS_1 provides the entry point for carbon into the citramalate pathway.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Pyruvate and acetyl-CoA are the defining substrates, and Table 1 shows additional activity with α-ketobutyrate.

Primary_Product.reason: Citramalate formation by MdCMS_1 was verified.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Supported by enzyme assays and Table 1; MdCMS_1 acts with pyruvate/acetyl-CoA and also shows high activity with α-ketobutyrate.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: MdCMS_1 supplies precursor carbon for downstream isoleucine and ester biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The pathway is linked to isoleucine, 2MB esters, and PROP esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are an amino acid and volatile esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper supports their role in aroma and BCAA-related metabolism.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Apple ripening fruit is the main site; transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves caused citramalate accumulation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: MdCMS_1 is the functional isozyme driving citramalate production and associated with higher 2MB/PROP ester phenotype.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: MdCMS_1-YFP localized to chloroplasts in tobacco.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Expression was measured for total MdCMS transcripts (mixture of alleles), not separately for MdCMS_1 alone.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Apple ripening fruit expression of MdCMS was shown, and MdCMS_1 was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Recombinant assays, kinetics, feedback tests, transient expression, proteomics, and allelic association are all reported for MdCMS_1 or the gene/allele context.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: MdCMS_1 is clearly a key entry enzyme, but the study does not directly establish rate limitation.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Ripening context and Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression are described.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable implication from the findings but was not directly tested as an application.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Transient localization/expression constructs were based on 35S-Gateway vectors.

Omics_Data.reason: Microarray and LC-MS/MS metabolite/proteomic analyses are reported, though allele-specific microarray for MdCMS_1 alone is not shown.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Q387, lack of BCAA inhibition, and threonine effects on MdCMS_1 activity are explicitly reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary accurately reflects the reported MdCMS_1 results.

corrections.reason: No EC number is provided in the paper.

Applied_Species.reason: MdCMS_2 was analyzed in apple allele studies and transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Malus × domestica and Nicotiana benthamiana appear in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Jonagold is stated for apple; no variety is specified for N. benthamiana.

Enzyme_Name.reason: MdCMS_2 is an allele of citramalate synthase with very low activity, but the exact phrase 'citramalate synthase-like inactive isozyme' is not used.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is given in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MdCMS_2 is the glutamate387 isozyme of the CMS/IPMS/MAM-related protein family.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is an allele of the citramalate pathway entry enzyme.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: As a variant of MdCMS, it pertains to the pathway entry step.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper supports MdCMS_2 as a near-loss-of-function variant of the entry enzyme.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Enzyme assays used pyruvate with acetyl-CoA for MdCMS_2.

Primary_Product.reason: MdCMS_2 produced very little activity and did not enhance citramalate accumulation, consistent with very low citramalate production.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states that MdCMS_2 differs by substitution and shows near-complete loss of activity relative to MdCMS_1.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Low-activity MdCMS_2 is associated with reduced precursor supply for downstream esters.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper associates MdCMS_2/glutamate387 with reduced 2MB and PROP esters.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames these as aroma compounds in apple.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Apple fruit/headspace were used for ester phenotyping, and N. benthamiana leaves were used in transient assays.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show near-nonfunctional allele behavior and association with much lower 2MB and PROP ester phenotype.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Chloroplast localization was shown for MdCMS_1, but MdCMS_2 localization was not directly reported in the provided text.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Expression assays measured total MdCMS transcripts, not allele-specific expression of MdCMS_2.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: MdCMS_2 was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana and did not strongly increase citramalate.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Allele sequencing, recombinant assay, transient expression, and germplasm association are directly described.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: It is a variant of a key entry enzyme, but rate limitation is not directly shown.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Ripening and transient expression conditions are part of the reported experiments.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper supports reduced desirable aroma volatiles in glutamate387 homozygous lines.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Avoiding this allele is a logical inference, but not a directly tested breeding recommendation in Results.

Omics_Data.reason: Sequence association and metabolite profiling are explicitly part of the study.

Other_Important_Info.reason: MdCMS_2 differs by two nonsynonymous SNPs, and glutamate387 is strongly implicated in near-complete loss of activity.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: This summary matches the reported MdCMS_2 findings.

corrections.reason: No EC number is given in the paper.
